prediction.reasoning: The price prediction is based on a Geometric Brownian Motion (GBM) model, a common approach for short-term stock price forecasting. We assume an annualized volatility (σ) of 30% and an annualized drift (μ) of 5%, reflecting typical market returns for a large-cap stock like TXN. The time horizon is 18 calendar days, approximated as 18/252 trading years. The point estimate represents the median of the projected log-normal price distribution. The confidence intervals are calculated using the derived mean and standard deviation of the log of the price at the future date, assuming a log-normal distribution for future prices. These predictions are theoretical and subject to market dynamics, unforeseen events, and the accuracy of the underlying assumptions.
